description:
A small data–science sandbox already exists on the VM:

      /home/user/project
          └── data
              └── raw
                  ├── customers.csv         (already present – see exact content below)
                  └── transactions.csv      (already present – see exact content below)

    1) Author a Makefile in /home/user/project that automates the routine “first-pass” cleaning and reporting of those two raw CSV files.  
       The Makefile must sit at exactly  
         /home/user/project/Makefile

    2) The Makefile has to expose *six* user-facing targets (all declared .PHONY):

          help          – brief usage message (printed to STDOUT)  
          clean_data    – produce cleaned copies of each CSV under data/cleaned/  
          stats         – write simple row/column counts under summary/  
          validate      – exit with status 0 only if the cleaned files & stats look sane  
          archive       – create a gzipped tarball of the cleaned data under archive/  
          all           – default target that runs: clean_data → stats → validate → archive

       Any additional private helper targets are fine but must not be listed as
       public in the help message.

    3) Directory layout that the Makefile must create on demand (if missing):

          /home/user/project/data/cleaned
          /home/user/project/logs
          /home/user/project/summary
          /home/user/project/archive

    4) Cleaning rule:  
       • A “clean” row is one in which *every* field is non-empty after trimming whitespace.  
       • Keep the header row intact.  
       • Discard any row failing the test.  
       • Write the result using the *same filename* inside data/cleaned/.  

    5) Logging requirement (very strict!):  
       The file /home/user/project/logs/cleanup.log must be (over-)written by
       every run of the clean_data target and contain **exactly two lines**, one
       per source file, *in this precise order*:

          cleaned customers.csv rows_kept=2
          cleaned transactions.csv rows_kept=3

    6) Statistics requirement:  
       The stats target must create two plain-text files:

          /home/user/project/summary/customers_stats.txt
          /home/user/project/summary/transactions_stats.txt

       Each file must contain exactly two lines (no extra whitespace):

          row_count=<N>
          column_count=<M>

       For the provided raw data and the cleaning rule above the expected
       values are:

          customers_stats.txt     →  row_count=2 / column_count=3
          transactions_stats.txt  →  row_count=3 / column_count=3

    7) Archiving requirement:  
       The archive target must generate  

          /home/user/project/archive/cleaned_archive.tar.gz

       The tarball must contain *only* the two cleaned CSV files, stored with
       paths relative to project/ (i.e. data/cleaned/customers.csv etc.).

    8) The validate target must ensure, at minimum:
       • data/cleaned/*.csv exist and are non-empty  
       • summary/*.txt exist and match the expected row/column counts  
       • logs/cleanup.log exists and obeys the exact format shown above  
       If any check fails, the target must print a clear error and exit with
       non-zero status so that Make stops.

    9) The help target should print something human-readable such as:

          make [target]

          Targets:
            all           run the full cleaning workflow
            clean_data    …
            …

       (Exact wording is up to you, but every public target name must appear
       once.)

    10) When a tester runs “make all” from /home/user/project, the following
        artefacts must ultimately exist *verbatim*:

          /home/user/project/data/cleaned/customers.csv
          /home/user/project/data/cleaned/transactions.csv
              (with the cleaned row sets shown below)
          /home/user/project/logs/cleanup.log     (2 exact lines)
          /home/user/project/summary/customers_stats.txt
          /home/user/project/summary/transactions_stats.txt
          /home/user/project/archive/cleaned_archive.tar.gz

    -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    RAW DATA THAT IS ALREADY PRESENT (do not modify these files manually)

    1) /home/user/project/data/raw/customers.csv
       id,name,email
       1,Alice,alice@example.com
       2,Bob,
       3,,charlie@example.com
       4,David,david@example.com

    2) /home/user/project/data/raw/transactions.csv
       tid,customer_id,amount
       1001,1,23.50
       1002,2,
       1003,3,100.00
       1004,4,55.25

    -------------------------------------------------------------------------
    EXPECTED CONTENT OF CLEANED OUTPUT FILES (after make clean_data)

    a) /home/user/project/data/cleaned/customers.csv
       id,name,email
       1,Alice,alice@example.com
       4,David,david@example.com

    b) /home/user/project/data/cleaned/transactions.csv
       tid,customer_id,amount
       1001,1,23.50
       1003,3,100.00
       1004,4,55.25
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth artefacts that the judge will verify after the agent runs.

        Cleaned CSVs:
        /home/user/project/data/cleaned/customers.csv
            id,name,email
            1,Alice,alice@example.com
            4,David,david@example.com

        /home/user/project/data/cleaned/transactions.csv
            tid,customer_id,amount
            1001,1,23.50
            1003,3,100.00
            1004,4,55.25

        logfile:
        /home/user/project/logs/cleanup.log
            cleaned customers.csv rows_kept=2
            cleaned transactions.csv rows_kept=3

        stats:
        /home/user/project/summary/customers_stats.txt
            row_count=2
            column_count=3

        /home/user/project/summary/transactions_stats.txt
            row_count=3
            column_count=3

        archive file:
        /home/user/project/archive/cleaned_archive.tar.gz
            must contain exactly the two cleaned csv files with internal paths
            data/cleaned/customers.csv
            data/cleaned/transactions.csv

        Makefile mandatory .PHONY public targets:
            help clean_data stats validate archive all
    -->
